name: Node.js Project Structure
description: Organize a Node.js API project by domain/feature, not by layer. Each feature folder owns its routes, controller, service, and types.
template:
## Node.js Project Structure (Feature-First)

```
src/
├── features/
│   ├── users/
│   │   ├── users.router.ts      # Express/Fastify routes
│   │   ├── users.controller.ts  # HTTP layer — parse req, call service, send res
│   │   ├── users.service.ts     # Business logic
│   │   ├── users.repository.ts  # DB queries
│   │   ├── users.schema.ts      # Zod validation schemas
│   │   └── users.types.ts       # TypeScript interfaces
│   └── auth/
│       ├── auth.router.ts
│       ├── auth.controller.ts
│       └── auth.service.ts
├── shared/
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   ├── authenticate.ts      # JWT verification
│   │   ├── validate.ts          # Zod request validation
│   │   ├── rateLimiter.ts
│   │   └── errorHandler.ts
│   ├── db/
│   │   └── client.ts            # Prisma / Drizzle client
│   ├── config.ts                # env vars + validation
│   └── errors.ts                # custom error classes
├── app.ts                       # Express app setup
└── server.ts                    # HTTP server + graceful shutdown
```

**Rules:**
- Controller = thin adapter between HTTP and service. No business logic.
- Service = pure business logic. No HTTP concepts (req/res).
- Repository = all DB access. No business logic.
- Shared middleware lives in `shared/` — not inside feature folders.

name: Request Validation with Zod
description: Validate every incoming request body, query, and params against a Zod schema at the route level. Reject invalid requests before they reach business logic.

name: Centralized Error Handling Middleware
description: Use a single Express error middleware at the bottom of the app to handle all errors uniformly. Never send stack traces to clients in production.

name: Graceful Shutdown
description: On SIGTERM/SIGINT, stop accepting new connections, drain in-flight requests, close DB connections, then exit. Prevents data corruption on deploys.

name: Environment Configuration with Zod
description: Parse and validate all environment variables at startup. Fail fast with clear error messages if required env vars are missing or malformed.

name: Rate Limiting with express-rate-limit
description: Apply rate limiting to all API routes. Use stricter limits for authentication endpoints to prevent credential stuffing attacks.

name: JWT Authentication Pattern
description: Use short-lived access tokens (15m) with refresh tokens (7d) stored in HttpOnly cookies. Never store JWT in localStorage.

name: Node.js Streams for Large Data
description: Use readable streams and pipe() to process large files or responses without loading everything into memory. Critical for file uploads, CSV processing, and proxying.

name: Health Check and Readiness Endpoints
description: Expose /health (liveness) and /ready (readiness) endpoints for container orchestration. Liveness checks if the process is alive; readiness checks if it can serve traffic.

name: API Versioning Strategy
description: Version APIs via URL path prefix (/api/v1/). Maintain backward compatibility for one major version. Deprecate with response headers before removal.
